title: Getting random 403 Forbidden errors on the dashboard
body: I keep getting 403 errors when I try to access the TaskFlow dashboard. It's not every time — sometimes it loads fine, sometimes I get a 403 page. It seems totally random. My permissions haven't changed and other people on my team say they're seeing it too. Started happening a couple days ago.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for reporting this — intermittent 403 errors are frustrating, especially when permissions haven't changed.

To help us narrow this down: could you walk us through the exact steps you're taking when the 403 appears? For example:

- Are you logging in fresh, or are you already logged in when it happens?
- Is it the main dashboard URL you're hitting directly, or are you navigating there from another page?
- When it fails with a 403, does refreshing the page fix it, or does it stay broken for a while?

Any pattern you've noticed — even if it feels unreliable — would be really helpful.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: I'm usually already logged in when it happens. I just go to the dashboard URL like normal — nothing special. The weird thing is that if I get a 403 and just hit refresh, sometimes it works on the next try. It's like every few attempts it fails. I'd say maybe 1 out of 3 times it gives me the error? I honestly thought it was some kind of network issue at first. It started around the same time I got moved to a new role — I'm an 'analyst' now — but I figured that was just a coincidence since my team lead said the permissions were all set up.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for those details — the timing with your role change is a really useful clue, even if it turns out to be coincidental.

Could you share a few environment details?

- What **browser** are you using (and version, if you know it)?
- What **OS** are you on (e.g., Windows 11, macOS Sonoma, etc.)?
- Do you know which **version of TaskFlow** your team is running, or whether it's the cloud-hosted or self-hosted deployment?

This will help us figure out whether something like session caching or a load balancer inconsistency might be involved.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: I'm on Chrome, not sure of the exact version — whatever the latest is, I think? I'm running Windows 11. As for TaskFlow, I believe we're on version 2.3.1 — it's self-hosted, our IT team manages it. I'm not really sure about the load balancer stuff but I know we have some kind of setup like that.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: [RESOLVED] {"title":"Intermittent 403 Forbidden on dashboard after role change to 'analyst' (self-hosted, load-balanced)","problem":"Users with the 'analyst' role intermittently receive 403 Forbidden errors when accessing the TaskFlow dashboard. The issue affects approximately 1 in 3 page loads, and refreshing sometimes resolves it. Multiple team members who were moved to the same role are experiencing the issue. It began a couple of days ago, coinciding with role reassignments.","root_cause_hypothesis":"In a load-balanced self-hosted deployment, the 'analyst' role permissions are likely inconsistent across backend instances. When a request hits an instance with stale or incomplete permission configuration for the new role, it returns 403. This explains the intermittent nature (round-robin or random LB routing) and the correlation with the role change. Possible causes: (1) permission/RBAC cache not invalidated uniformly across instances after the role was configured, (2) the role was only configured on some instances or database replicas, or (3) a session-sticky vs. round-robin LB mismatch causing some requests to hit uncached authorization paths.","reproduction_steps":["Log in to TaskFlow as a user with the 'analyst' role","Navigate to the main dashboard URL","Observe that the page loads successfully","Refresh the page repeatedly (approximately 5-10 times)","Observe that roughly 1 in 3 attempts returns a 403 Forbidden page","Note that refreshing again after a 403 often succeeds"],"environment":"Chrome (latest), Windows 11, TaskFlow 2.3.1, self-hosted deployment with load balancer","severity":"high","impact":"Multiple users on the 'analyst' role are blocked from reliably accessing the dashboard, disrupting their workflow. Approximately 33% of page loads fail. This affects the entire team that was recently reassigned to the 'analyst' role.","recommended_fix":"1. Check permission/RBAC configuration for the 'analyst' role across all backend instances behind the load balancer. 2. Inspect whether authorization caches are being invalidated consistently across instances when roles are updated. 3. Compare the permission grants for 'analyst' on each instance or database replica. 4. Check load balancer session affinity settings — if round-robin, a per-instance cache inconsistency would produce exactly this pattern. 5. As a quick mitigation, restart all backend instances to force a fresh permission cache load.","proposed_test_case":"Assign a test user to the 'analyst' role, then make 20+ sequential requests to the dashboard endpoint through the load balancer and verify all return 200. Repeat after clearing caches and restarting individual instances to confirm consistency.","information_gaps":["Server-side access logs showing which backend instance serves the 403 vs. 200 responses","Exact load balancer configuration (round-robin, sticky sessions, etc.)","Whether other roles are also affected or only 'analyst'","Whether the 'analyst' role existed before or was newly created when users were reassigned"]}
